US and Iran consider a ceasefire in exchange for reopening the Strait of Hormuz; Trump has briefed Saudi Arabia and the UAE

items.headline: Iran fires on Israel and Gulf neighbors as Trump claims threat from Tehran nearly eliminated
items.summary: Iran's attacks on Gulf states along with its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz have disrupted the world's energy supplies with effects far beyond the Middle East. That has proved to be Iran's greatest strategic advantage in the war.

items.headline: UK Hosts 40-Nation Talks to Reopen Iran-Blocked Strait of Hormuz as Oil Prices Surge Amid War
items.summary: LONDON — Britain convened a virtual summit Thursday with about 40 countries to explore ways of reopening the Strait of Hormuz, the vital Persian Gulf chokepoint effectively shut down by Iran during the ongoing U.S.-Israeli war, as global oil prices climbed on…

items.headline: US military vs Iran's 'arch defence': Dangerous new phase of war looms
items.summary: While Donald Trump talks about ending and winning the war in Iran, major US military assets are still on their way to the Middle East. These are the options they give him.

items.headline: The Critical Commodities Caught in the Hormuz Blockade
items.summary: When an Indian tanker carrying liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) passed through the Strait of Hormuz recently, its progress was followed by excited live TV news coverage. “Its position was received eight minutes ago. It’s currently at 12.5 knots and is moving at …

items.headline: Oil Spikes As Trump Vows To Hit Iran “Extremely Hard Over Next 2-3 Weeks”
items.summary: Trump declares 'core strategic objectives met', threatens 2-3 more weeks of bombing, no mention of ceasefire

items.headline: What is Benjamin Netanyahu’s end game in the Iran war?
items.summary: The war in the Middle East is a huge political book for the Israeli prime minister in an election year – but it may come at a great cost.

items.headline: Putin Is Not Trapped: Why Regime Survival Does Not Depend on Victory
items.summary: For four years, analysts and policymakers have warned that Russian President Vladimir Putin “cannot afford to lose” in Ukraine. Increasingly, some argue he cannot afford peace either. In this view, the war is existential for his regime. Defeat would shatter P…

items.headline: Will China join Pakistan-led efforts to mediate US-Iran peace?
items.summary: Pakistan's Deputy PM Ishaq Dar visits China after Islamabad talks to broker negotiations between the US and Iran.

items.headline: The war with Iran may be ushering in a new nuclear age
items.summary: Global tensions are rising as nations consider developing nuclear weapons. Donald Trump's policies are prompting countries like Germany and Poland to explore their own nuclear deterrents. Meanwhile, China and Russia are concerned about proliferation in Japan …

items.headline: Oil prices surge while Asian share prices rise moderately
items.summary: Oil prices spike amid Iran war concerns as Asian markets show modest gains in cautious trading.

items.headline: World anxious to open Hormuz Strait while Trump and Iran trade threats
items.summary: Dozens of countries sought ways to restart vital energy shipments through the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday after U.S. President Donald Trump vowed more aggressive strikes on Iran, sending oil prices higher again and deepening strain on consumers. In a speech …

items.headline: US Burned 14 Years of Missiles in 30 Days
items.summary: The US fired 850 Tomahawk missiles in 30 days but buys only 57 a year. As munitions run dry, the Pentagon pivots to ground troops in Iran, guaranteeing the Strait of Hormuz stays closed, European gas storage hits crisis levels, and Western energy prices keep …

items.headline: Trump's Iran-war address caused another oil spike. Here's what smart people in markets are saying.
items.summary: Business Insider gathered commentary from top expects as oil prices climbed on Thursday after Trump's remarks on the war in Iran.

items.summary:

items.headline: Deterrent or danger: After Iran, are Saudi Arabia and Turkey next in line for nuclear weapons?
items.summary: The nuclear threat in the Middle East is growing due to the Iran war, and not just because nuclear sites in Iran and Israel are being attacked. Other countries may decide they're safer with a nuclear weapon too.

items.headline: HMA Moscow Nigel Casey's interview on UK-Russia relations
items.summary: British Ambassador to Moscow Nigel Casey gave an interview about the state and prospects of relations with Russia to RBC outlet on Friday 27 March 2026.

items.headline: Middle East War: Fresh satellite images show destruction at Iran’s Qeshm port after US-Israeli attack
items.summary: Recent satellite imagery reveals significant destruction to port infrastructure on Qeshm Island, Iran, following coordinated strikes by the US and Israel. Additionally, a critical bridge in Karaj has suffered damage, leading to civilian loss of life.

items.headline: Iran and proxies fire fewer total projectiles, increase accuracy in attacks on region (March 28–April 1 updates)
items.summary: Between March 28 and April 1, Iran and its allied militias continued launching drones, missiles, and rockets against regional countries—with less overall frequency but more accuracy. The attacks targeted energy infrastructure, airports, military facilities, p…

items.headline: Trump threatens to destroy Iran's desalination plants. Here’s what that could mean for the Mideast
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump on Monday threatened to target Iran’s energy infrastructure, including the country's desalination plants. Such a move — and Iran's possible targeting of the plants of its Gulf Arab neighbors — could have devastating impacts across …

items.headline: Crude Slides as War Exit Signals Emerge
items.summary: Oil declined as Iran and the US signaled potential de-escalation despite ongoing supply disruptions.

items.headline: Satellite images begin to show damage wrought by Iran war
items.summary: Satellite images are emerging, offering a view of the Iran war's damage. Ships are seen burning in an Iranian port. Military bases in the region show destroyed buildings. Information on the war's toll has been limited. High-resolution images from various firm…

items.headline: What to know about U.S. shifting targets, new deployments and diplomatic double game as conflict enters Day 30?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran that began with targeted "decapitation" strikes on Feb. 28 has spiralled into a multi-front regional war with no end in sight.

items.headline: The Latest: Iranian-backed Houthi rebels claim responsibility for missile attacks on Israel
items.summary: Iranian-backed Houthi rebels have claimed responsibility for two missile attacks on Israel as the war in the Middle East marks its one-month anniversary...

items.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei's Whereabouts Spark Crisis as IRGC Controls Presidential Access
items.summary: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian faces political deadlock as the IRGC controls information flow between him and Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, who remains unseen since March. The military has blocked presidential appointments while establishing a security perimeter around power centers, raising questions about the new Supreme Leader's health and effective control.

dimension_signal.rationale: Physical absence and inability to make public appearances while military controls access suggests weakened position

items.headline: IRGC Intelligence Threatened Assembly of Experts' Families to Secure Mojtaba's Leadership
items.summary: IranWire reports that IRGC intelligence pressured members of Iran's Assembly of Experts and threatened their families to secure Mojtaba Khamenei's appointment as Supreme Leader. Sources indicate the pressure was so intense that dissenting members were forced into supporting him through psychological and political coercion.

dimension_signal.rationale: Installation through coercion and threats rather than legitimate process undermines institutional legitimacy

items.headline: China and Pakistan Launch Five-Point Peace Initiative for Iran War
items.summary: Pakistani Foreign Minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar met Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi in Beijing to announce a new initiative for ending the Iran war. The plan includes immediate ceasefire, securing the Strait of Hormuz, and UN-backed negotiations, marking China's formal entry into mediating the conflict.

items.headline: Saudi Crown Prince Denied Urging Trump to Strike Iran Despite US-Israel War Inception
items.summary: The Washington Post reports that MBS conducted multiple phone calls with Trump urging attacks on Iran, stating Iran would become stronger if Washington didn't strike immediately. Trump's decision to attack Iran on February 28 came after Saudi and Israeli governments repeatedly lobbied him, though Saudi Arabia officially denies these reports.

dimension_signal.rationale: Repeatedly pushing for immediate military action demonstrates impulsive decision-making pattern

items.headline: IAEA Reports Iran Natanz Nuclear Site Inaccessible After US Strikes
items.summary: The IAEA confirmed that recent bombings failed to destroy the Natanz nuclear facility but significant damage to entrance buildings has made it inaccessible. The agency has been unable to account for Iran's enriched uranium stockpile for over eight months following withdrawal of inspectors in June 2025.

items.headline: E3 Statement Confirms Iran Only Non-Nuclear State Producing 60% Enriched Uranium
items.summary: France, Germany and the UK delivered joint statement to IAEA Board confirming Iran remains the only state without nuclear weapons to have produced 60% High Enriched Uranium. The E3 expressed deep concern about lack of transparency over Iran's nuclear program with no credible civilian justification for enrichment activities.

items.headline: Turkey's Erdogan Warns Iran Against Missile Strikes While Seeking Mediation Role
items.summary: Turkish President Erdogan expressed Turkey's resolve to stay out of the Iran conflict while cautioning Iran against persistence in error after NATO downed Iranian ballistic missiles over Turkish airspace. Foreign Minister Fidan urged Iran to be careful and avoid further launches toward Turkey while positioning Turkey as mediator.

items.headline: Istanbul Mayor Imamoglu Arrested as Erdogan Reshapes Political Field
items.summary: Turkish President Erdogan's main rival, Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu, was arrested on March 19 despite ongoing Kurdish peace initiative. The arrest demonstrates Erdogan's use of state institutions to reshape the political field while pursuing constitutional changes to remove term limits for 2028 elections.

dimension_signal.rationale: Arrest of main political rival demonstrates prioritizing power retention over democratic norms

items.headline: China Withholds Support for Pakistan Railway Project During Sharif Beijing Visit
items.summary: China withheld support for the Main Line-1 railway upgrade, a flagship CPEC project, during Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif's September 2025 visit to Beijing. Instead, agreements worth $8.5 billion were signed in other sectors, with analysts noting the move comes amid Islamabad's engagement with Washington and broader geopolitical realignments.

items.headline: Chinese Foreign Ministry Confirms Mediation Between Pakistan and Afghanistan
items.summary: Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning confirmed China has made mediation efforts in its own way following the Pakistan-Afghanistan conflict escalation, maintaining close communication with both sides via multiple channels. China provided platforms for dialogue between both countries, with consultations being implemented steadily.
items.source: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of China

items.headline: UN Security Council Debates Iran Nuclear Program Amid Snapback Sanctions Dispute
items.summary: The Security Council met to debate Iran's nuclear program as Russia and China challenged the legality of UN sanctions on Tehran under the snapback mechanism. Multiple countries expressed support for IAEA cooperation and concerns over Iran's nuclear program, with calls for full adherence to international obligations.
